devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । भरतस्यात्मजः सुमतिर्नामाभिहितो यमु ह वाव केचित्पाखण्डिन ऋषभ पदवीमनुवर्तमानं चानार्या अवेदसमाम्नातां देवतां स्वमनीषया पापी...

translation:
Śrī Śuka continued:
As already narrated (supra 7.3), Bharata had a son called Sumati. He followed the path of Ṛṣabha. In the Kali Age, heretics (non-believers in the Vedas) outside the Aryan fold, will set him up, according to their wicked and perverted intelligence, as a deity not described in the Vedic texts. (They will think him to be an incarnation of Lord Buddha).
By (queen) Vṛddhasenā, he had a son by name Devajit (conqueror of gods).

devanagari: अथासुर्यां तत्तनयो देवद्युम्नस्ततो धेनुमत्यां सुतः परमेष्ठी तस्य सुवर्चलायां प्रतीह उपजातः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: athāsuryāṃ tattanayo devadyumnastato dhenumatyāṃ sutaḥ parameṣṭhī tasya suvarcalāyāṃ pratīha upajātaḥ
translation: Then, through Āsurī (Devajit’s wife) was born to him a son, Devadyumna; he had by Dhenumatī, a son, called Parameṣṭhī; and then Parameṣṭhī begot on Sauvarcalā a son called Pratīha.

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Having taught the knowledge of ātman. (to many). he himself became thoroughly purified, and directly realized the Supreme Soul.

devanagari: प्रतीहात्सुवर्चलायां प्रतिहर्त्रादयस्त्रय आसन्निज्याकोविदाः सूनवः प्रतिहर्तुः स्तुत्यामजभूमानावजनिषाताम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: pratīhātsuvarcalāyāṃ pratihartrādayastraya āsannijyākovidāḥ sūnavaḥ pratihartuḥ stutyāmajabhūmānāvajaniṣātām

translation: From Pratīha, through (his wife, another) Suvarcalā, three sons beginning with Pratihartā were born; all of them were well-versed in the sacrificial lore. Pratihartā begot on (his wife) Stuti, two sons Aja and Bhūman.

devanagari: भूम्न ऋषिकुल्यायामुद्गीथस्ततः प्रस्तावो देवकुल्यायां प्रस्तावान्नियुत्सायां हृदयज । आसीद्विभुर्विभो रत्यां च पृथुषेणस्तस्मान्नक्त । आकूत...
english_devnagari: bhūmna ṛṣikulyāyāmudgīthastataḥ prastāvo devakulyāyāṃ prastāvānniyutsāyāṃ hṛdayaja āsīdvibhurvibho ratyāṃ ca pṛthuṣeṇastasmānnakta ākūtyāṃ jajñe naktāddrutiputro gayo rājarṣipravara udāraśravā ajāyata sākṣādbhagavato viṣṇorjagadrirakṣiṣayā gṛhītasattvasya kalātmavattvādilakṣaṇena mahāpuruṣatāṃ prāptaḥ

translation: Bhūman had by Ṛṣikulyā a son called Udgītha. He had (a son named) Prastāva from Devakulyā. Prastāva had a son called Vibhu, through (queen) Niyutsā; Vibhu had Pṛthuṣeṇa from Rati; Pṛthuṣeṇa begot Nakta from Ākūtī. From Nakta and (his wife) Druti was born the eminent royal sage Gaya of noble reputation. Gaya who attained the status of a Mahāpuruṣa (an exalted personage) by his possession of characteristics (of greatness) such as self-control, was an aṃśa (part or ray) of Lord Viṣṇu himself who, for the protection of the world, assumed a form consisting of pure Sattva.

devanagari: स वै स्वधर्मेण प्रजापालनपोषणप्रीणनोपलालनानुशासनलक्षणेनेज्यादिना च भगवति महापुरुषे परावरे ब्रह्मणि सर्वात्मनार्पितपरमार्थलक्षणेन ब्रह्मवि...

[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation:
He followed his righteous duty as a king by protecting his subjects, supplying them with the means of livelihood, providing them with (amenities of) recreation behaving tenderly with them and by governing them per legal precepts. He dedicated with absolute faith his sacrificial and other pious acts to the glorious great personage viz. the Brahman who is higher than the highest.
By constantly serving the feet of those who have realized Brahman and by cultivating path of devotion to the Lord, his mind and intellect became extremely pure. His mind became completely free from the false identification of the non-Soul (viz. the body and its belongings) with the Soul. Though he realized within him the identification of his Self with Brahman, he was totally free from pride and as such he protected the earth.
Oh descendant of Pāṇḍavas! Persons well-versed in Purāṇic history sing the following stanzas about him.

devanagari: गयं नृपः कः प्रतियाति कर्मभिर्यज्वाभिमानी बहुविद्धर्मगोप्ता । समागतश्रीः सदसस्पतिः सतां सत्सेवकोऽन्यो भगवत्कलामृते ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: gayaṃ nṛpaḥ kaḥ pratiyāti karmabhiryajvābhimānī bahuviddharmagoptā samāgataśrīḥ sadasaspatiḥ satāṃ satsevako
translation: “What other king, except the one in whom a ray of the Lord has been manifest, can equal Gaya in his achievements? Who else is there like him, a performer of great sacrifices, so highly respected by all, possessor of extensive scholarship, a defender of religion, one so highly blessed with the Goddess of Fortune, a leader of the assembly of the learned and a devoted servant of the saints.

devanagari: यमभ्यषिञ्चन्परया मुदा सतीः सत्याशिषो दक्षकन्याः सरिद्भिः । यस्य प्रजानां दुदुहे धराशिषो निराशिषो गुणवत्सस्नुतोधाः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: yamabhyaṣiñcanparayā mudā satīḥ satyāśiṣo dakṣakanyāḥ saridbhiḥ yasya prajānāṃ duduhe dharāśiṣo nirāśiṣo guṇavatsasnutodhāḥ

translation: The virtuous daughters of Dakṣa (such as Śradḍhā, Maitrī, Dayā) whose blessings always prove true, accompanied the presiding deities of holy rivers (like Gaṅgā) sprinkled him with water (at the time of his coronation) in ecstatic joy. The king, himself was free from desire. But just as the milk begins to flow out of the udders of a cow at the sight of its calf, so at the sight of his excellent virtues, the earth yielded like a cow, milk in the form of the desired objects of his subjects.

devanagari: छन्दांस्यकामस्य च यस्य कामान्दुदूहुराजह्रुरथो बलिं नृपाः । प्रत्यञ्चिता युधि धर्मेण विप्रा यदाशिषां षष्ठमंशं परेत्य ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: chandāṃsyakāmasya ca yasya kāmāndudūhurājahruratho baliṃ nṛpāḥ pratyañcitā yudhi dharmeṇa viprā yadāśiṣāṃ ṣaṣṭhamaṃśaṃ paretya
translation: Though he (Gaya) was above desires, Vedas and the rites prescribed therein blessed him with all enjoyments. (Inimical) kings who were honoured by him with a volley of arrows in the battle, brought him tributes. The Brāhmaṇas who were honoured by him with sumptuous sacrificial fees and donations while performing religious acts, allotted to him one-sixth of their total merit.

devanagari: यस्याध्वरे भगवानध्वरात्मा मघोनि माद्यत्युरुसोमपीथे । श्रद्धाविशुद्धाचलभक्तियोग समर्पितेज्याफलमाजहार ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yasyādhvare bhagavānadhvarātmā maghoni mādyatyurusomapīthe śraddhāviśuddhācalabhaktiyoga samarpitejyāphalamājahāra

translation: In his sacrifices, liberal libations of Soma juice were offered. While Indra (the king of gods) became intoxicated (with Soma juice), the glorious Lord who is the Soul of the Sacrifices appeared in person and accepted the fruit of the Sacrifice offered (by the king) in the spirit of unswerving devotion rendered absolutely pure by (unflinching) faith.

devanagari: यत्प्रीणनाद्बर्हिषि देवतिर्यङ् मनुष्यवीरुत्तृणमाविरिञ्चात् । प्रीयेत सद्यः स ह विश्वजीवः प्रीतः स्वयं प्रीतिमगाद्गयस्य ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: yatprīṇanādbarhiṣi devatiryaṅ manuṣyavīruttṛṇamāviriñcāt prīyeta sadyaḥ sa ha viśvajīvaḥ prītaḥ svayaṃ prītimagādgayasya

translation: By pleasing whom (i.e. the Lord) the whole universe from god Brahmā upto gods, men, sub-human beings, creepers and grass, become the Self-same Lord, the very Soul of the universe, who, though ever blissful by nature, became evidently satisfied and delighted at Gaya’s performance of sacrifice.

devanagari: गयाद्गयन्त्यां चित्ररथः सुगतिरवरोधन इति त्रयः पुत्रा बभूवुश्चित्ररथादूर्णायां सम्राडजनिष्ट तत उत्कलायां मरीचिर्मरीचेर्बिन्दुमत्यां बिन्...

translation: Gaya begot on Gayantī three sons, Citraratha, Sugati and Avarodhana. Out of them, Citraratha had by queen Ūrṇā a son called Samrāṭ.

devanagari: तत्रायं श्लोकः । प्रैयव्रतं वंशमिमं विरजश्चरमोद्भवः । अकरोदत्यलं कीर्त्या विष्णुः सुरगणं यथा ॥ १५ ॥

translation:
From Samrāṭ, thorough Utkalā, was born Marīci. From Marīci and Bindumatī was boṛn Bindumān; he had by Saraghā (a son) named Madhu; Madhu begot Vīravrata on Sumanas; from Vīravrata and queen Bhojā were born Manthu and Pramanthu; of Manthu, through Satyā, sprang Bhauvana; of Bhauvana and Dūṣaṇā was born Tvaṣṭā; from Tvaṣṭā through Virocanā came Viraja; and Viraja had by Viṣūcī a hundred sons, of whom Śatajit was the eldest, and also a daughter.
About Viraja is sung the following stanza:
